sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: c) A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is called { pronormal\/} if$H$and$H^g$are conjugate in$H,H^g$for every$g G$. We say that$H$is { strongly pronormal\/} if$L^g$is conjugate to a subgroup of$H$in$H,L^g$for every$L H$and$g G$. In a finite group, is a Hall subgroup with a Sylow tower always strongly pro\-normal? Notice that there exist finite (non-simple) groups with a non-pronormal Hall subgroup. Hall sub...
